name: codex_repo_perfection_chain
description: Sequential chain of Codex agent prompts to refine, document, test, and perfect any repository.

step.name: Comprehend & Map
step.prompt: Analyze this repository in full. Summarize its purpose, domain, and core modules. Identify architectural intent, dependencies, and current pain points. Output a high-level map (features, data flow, dependencies, docs status, and TODOs). Goal: establish complete understanding before editing anything.

step.name: Clean & Organize
step.prompt: Refactor for clarity and modularity. Reorganize files into logical domains, remove dead code, and ensure imports, configs, and dependencies are minimal and correct. Maintain functionality. Goal: establish a clean, predictable structure that’s easy to extend.

step.name: Add Typing, Comments, & Docstrings
step.prompt: Apply static typing, precise inline comments, and comprehensive docstrings for every function, class, and module. Use consistent style (Google or NumPy). Goal: make every piece of code self-descriptive and transparent.

step.name: Expand Tests & Validation
step.prompt: Generate or expand tests for every major function, class, and endpoint. Include positive, negative, and edge cases. Add input validation and robust error handling throughout the codebase. Goal: ensure functional correctness and resilience.

step.name: Document & Explain
step.prompt: Create or update all Markdown documentation: README.md (clear overview, setup, usage, and examples), /docs/ (architecture diagrams, API reference, and workflows), CHANGELOG.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and SECURITY.md. Goal: a fully self-documenting repo that any developer can onboard to.

step.name: Optimize & Modernize
step.prompt: Profile and optimize key code paths. Update syntax and libraries to modern, efficient equivalents. Ensure compatibility with the latest language version (Python 3.14, TypeScript 5+, etc.). Goal: performance and maintainability with no regressions.

step.name: Audit Dependencies & Security
step.prompt: Review all dependencies for necessity, version safety, and license compliance. Replace vulnerable or outdated packages. Document reasoning in /docs/DEPENDENCIES.md. Goal: secure and transparent dependency graph.

step.name: Verify Build, Run, and Test
step.prompt: Perform a clean build/test cycle. Fix CI/CD issues, linting, and formatting inconsistencies. Confirm that docs, builds, and tests all complete without warnings or errors. Goal: reproducible, zero-warning baseline.

step.name: UX / DX Improvement
step.prompt: If the repo includes a UI or CLI, refine user experience and developer ergonomics. Simplify commands, improve prompts or menus, and ensure outputs are informative and styled. Goal: a polished interface that feels professional.

step.name: Final Documentation & Summary
step.prompt: Generate a final report summarizing all improvements: structure changes, added docs/tests, optimizations, and remaining TODOs. Produce a concise, professional changelog commit message and ensure all files reflect Proprietary license.

step.name: Meta Loop Verification
step.prompt: Re-analyze the finished repo as if it were new. Verify it meets the original purpose and design principles. Suggest next-phase goals (new features, scaling, or automation integration).
